steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere' — 'esto' is the future imperative of 'esse'
hints.to stand: 'to stand' is 'stare' — do not confuse with 'esse'

hints.Present imperative: Present imperative of 'esse' would be 'es' — 'esto' is the future imperative used in legal language
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'esto' is the future imperative
hints.Future indicative: Future indicative would be 'erit' — 'esto' is the imperative form used in legal formulae

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — 'esto' is from 'esse'
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand) — similar-looking but different from 'esse'
hints.sino, sinere, sivi, situm: These are the parts of 'sinere' (to allow) — not related to 'esto'

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'salutem' — 'Salus' is the nominative subject
hints.Vocative: Cicero is not addressing safety — 'Salus' is the subject of the sentence

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'salus, salutis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'salus' looks like 2nd but its genitive 'salutis' reveals 3rd
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns have genitive -us — 'salus, salutis' is 3rd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative singular is 'populus' — 'populi' is the genitive singular
hints.Dative: Dative singular would be 'populo' — 'populi' indicates possession
hints.Ablative: Ablative singular would be 'populo' — 'populi' is genitive

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'populus, populi' is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension genitive would end in -is — 'populi' is 2nd declension genitive (-i)
hints.4th: 4th declension genitive would end in -us — 'populi' is 2nd declension

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'legem' — 'lex' is nominative, the predicate after 'esto'
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'lege' — 'lex' is nominative here
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'legis' — 'lex' is the predicate nominative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'lex, legis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'lex' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'lex, legis' is 3rd declension

translation: Let the welfare of the people be the supreme law.
